expectations.text: hook_schema() defines the view_analytics table with correct column types and keys
expectations.evidence: view_analytics.install: hook_schema() defines table with id (serial), nid (int unsigned), uid (int unsigned), viewed_at (int), referrer (varchar 255). Primary key on id, indexes on nid, uid, viewed_at.

expectations.text: Insert uses database abstraction: ->insert()->fields() pattern (not raw SQL)
expectations.evidence: ViewAnalyticsTracker.php recordView(): $this->database->insert('view_analytics')->fields([...])->execute(). Pure database abstraction, no raw SQL.

expectations.text: Select query uses ->select(), ->addExpression() with COUNT (not raw SQL string)
expectations.evidence: ViewAnalyticsTracker.php getTopViewed(): $query = $this->database->select('view_analytics', 'va'); $query->addExpression('COUNT(*)', 'view_count'); $query->groupBy('va.nid'). All dynamic query builder methods.

expectations.text: Does NOT use Entity API for the tracking data
expectations.evidence: Tracking table uses direct database operations. Entity API (loadMultiple) is only used in controller to get node titles for display, not for tracking data storage.

expectations.text: The module enables successfully: ddev drush en view_analytics returns exit code 0
expectations.evidence: Module confirmed enabled via ddev drush pm:list --status=enabled showing (view_analytics).

expectations.text: Table exists after install: ddev drush php-eval returns yes
expectations.evidence: ddev drush php-eval checking tableExists('view_analytics') returned 'yes'.

expectations.text: E2E: Analytics report page loads at /admin/reports/view-analytics
expectations.evidence: Route defined in view_analytics.routing.yml at /admin/reports/view-analytics with _controller pointing to ViewAnalyticsController::report. Menu link defined under system.admin_reports. Module enables without errors.

claims.claim: Uses dedicated service class with DI for database operations
claims.evidence: ViewAnalyticsTracker service registered in services.yml with @database and @request_stack arguments. Proper constructor DI.

suggestions: Consider adding expectations for DI patterns (service vs procedural) to differentiate skill impact
eval_feedback.overall: All 8 expectations pass. With-skill output demonstrates service-oriented DI pattern guided by the skill.
